qa.overall_assessment: BA spec accurately identifies all three issues with correct file paths, line numbers, and root causes. All 4 ACs are well-formed, verifiable, and aligned with the user-stated requirement. No scope narrowing, no missing investigation, no file path errors detected.

dimensions_evaluated.rationale: BA provides specific line-number citations for all three items. Item A: lines 124-127 (stage-all fallback) and 329-340 (orphan commit) verified — the code at those exact lines matches BA's description. Item B: lines 884-903 verified — the $-strip defense at 898-903 only removes '$' characters, does not decode \xHH/\0NNN/\uNNNN escapes. BA's Python shlex test output matches expected behavior. Item C: grep confirmed exactly 16 files contain the old token under tests/generated/. Hook lines 938,946 confirmed to emit 'bulk-commit-sentinel-write'.

User requirement covers three items: cross-session isolation gap, ANSI-C escape bypass, stale test token. BA's MoSCoW Must-Have M1-M4 maps 1:1 to the three items (M1+M2 for Item A, M3 for Item B, M4 for Item C). Won't Have items are reasonable exclusions (pretool-git-privilege-guard.py changes, docs/dev/ historical artifacts, GIT_INDEX_FILE isolation) and do not drop any user-stated requirement. No scope narrowing detected.

BA investigated all three code locations. For Item A, BA identified both escape hatches (stage-all fallback AND orphan commit path) rather than just one — this is thorough. For Item B, BA ran an actual Python test to confirm shlex behavior with ANSI-C escapes and enumerated all escape forms (\xHH, \0NNN, \NNN, \uNNNN, \UHHHHHHHH, standard C escapes). For Item C, BA identified the exact count (16 files) and listed all file paths. No skipped investigation steps.

All file paths verified: agents/changelog-analyst.md exists and contains the described code at the cited lines. hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh exists with WRITE_ACTION_FLAGS and $-strip defense at lines 890-903. All 16 test files under tests/generated/ exist and contain 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write'. The file list in the spec matches the grep results exactly (same 16 files).

AC1 check method (grep for ABORT and scope_violation, verify absence of 'falling back to stage-all') directly matches the literal AC text. AC2 check method (subprocess invocation with test vectors, expected exit 2, expected stderr token) aligns with the AC's GIVEN/WHEN/THEN. AC3 check method (grep -r for old and new tokens) is the literal verification recipe stated in the AC text. AC4 check method (grep for BULK=false orphan skip behavior) matches the AC text. No drift between AC literal text and verification recipe.

acceptance_criteria_review.notes: Clear GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure. Check method is a simple grep that can be automated. The absent_pattern check ('falling back to stage-all') is a good negative assertion.
Test vectors cover hex (\x2d), leading-zero octal (\055), bare octal (\55), and provides a delete variant. The expected_exit and expected_stderr_token are concrete and measurable.
Dual grep check (zero old-token matches + 16 new-token matches) is a complete verification. Scope restricted to tests/generated/ to avoid touching historical docs.
Addresses the second escape hatch (orphan commit) separately from AC1 (stage-all fallback). BULK=false guard requirement is clear.
